title: nie-bot: script execution hook module (shlex + Command::new)
description:
What: Execute user-configured shell hook on message events, safely, without shell injection.

Algorithm:
1. Create bot/src/scripting.rs with pub async fn run_hook(cmd_str: &str, env_vars: &[(String,String)]) -> Result<BotEvent::ScriptOutput equivalent>:
   a. shlex::split(cmd_str) → None means unmatched quotes → Err(BotError::Config("hook: unmatched quotes in: ..."))
   b. argv.is_empty() → Err(BotError::Config("hook: empty command"))
   c. spawn: tokio::process::Command::new(&argv[0]).args(&argv[1..]).envs(env_vars).stdin(Stdio::null()).stdout(Stdio::piped()).stderr(Stdio::piped()).spawn()?
   d. timeout: tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(30), child.wait_with_output()).await → Err(_) → kill child + Err(BotError::ScriptTimeout)
   e. cap stdout at 65536 bytes; truncate silently if exceeded
   f. return (exit_code, stdout_string, stderr_string)

Defensive:
- MUST use shlex::split + Command::new — NEVER sh -c (injection boundary per CLAUDE.md)
- stdin: Stdio::null() — hook cannot read bot's stdin
- No env vars that contain keyfile path or daemon token
- Timeout 30s hard default (no config override needed for Phase 4f)

Agent team: I (implementer). V (validator) runs cargo build + clippy. Tests in Wave 4.
Tests: see W4B
acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p nie-bot clean; uses shlex::split + Command::new (grep -n sh -c must return nothing); clippy clean
